metadata.title: I Let Ai Sound Like Me Heres What

metadata.note: HAI Fellows batch v2 — Teardown register, @NikBearBrown, Remotion animated bookends. Credit: Yatra Rawat — Humanitarians AI Fellow. HONESTY: all numbers trace to source article.

beats.narration_text: Let's recap with Claude. Using Claude to draft your content can produce writing that mimics you but rings false. The failure sounds like a press release wearing a smile: polished warmth with nothing specific underneath. Decide what you're going for first, then listen for the wrong note that betrays it. One honest, specific detail restores the intimacy that generic AI phrasing strips away.

props.artifactTitle: When AI sounds almost like you
artifactLines: Using Claude to draft your content can produce writing that mimics you but rings false.
The failure sounds like a press release wearing a smile: polished warmth with nothing specific underneath.
Decide what you're going for first, then listen for the wrong note that betrays it.
One honest, specific detail restores the intimacy that generic AI phrasing strips away.

beats.narration_text: Letting Claude draft in my voice scared me because the result read 'like a press release wearing a smile' instead of like me. I want to learn to hear the wrong note and swap it for one honest, specific detail. Take a paragraph an AI wrote in my voice and (1) mark the exact phrases that sound like warmth performed rather than felt, (2) replace one of them with a concrete detail only I would know, and (3) tell me what tone I was actually aiming for.
